title: Supreme Court Declares Right to Walk on Footpaths a Fundamental Right
summary: The Supreme Court of India has ruled that the right to walk on footpaths is a fundamental right, linking pedestrian access to the right to life under Article 21 of the Constitution. The court observed that footpaths are essential public infrastructure and called for a dedicated law to ensure pedestrian rights across all roads in the country. The bench emphasised that denying safe walkways violates citizens' basic rights and directed authorities to ensure footpaths on all roads. The court also expressed concern about encroachments and poor maintenance of footpaths in urban and rural areas.
exam_angle: Directly relevant to expansive judicial interpretation of Article 21 (Right to Life) and the doctrine of implied fundamental rights; also connects to urban governance and the role of judiciary in enforcing socio-economic rights.
key_facts: Right to walk on footpaths declared a Fundamental Right by Supreme Court
Basis: Article 21 (Right to Life and Personal Liberty)
Court called for a dedicated legislation to protect pedestrian rights
Footpath encroachments and poor upkeep cited as rights violations
source: The Hindu / The Business Standard / India Today

title: India–US Trade Talks: India Reportedly Steps Back Amid Tariff Disputes
summary: A former senior official has stated that India has 'walked away' from ongoing trade negotiations with the United States due to disagreements over tariffs. This development comes amid broader US tariff measures under the Trump administration that have affected multiple trading partners. The remark underscores the difficulty of concluding a bilateral trade deal between India and the US, which has been under negotiation for several years. President Trump separately acknowledged that PM Modi's long tenure in office has benefited US trade prospects with India.
exam_angle: Relevant to India–US bilateral trade relations, WTO commitments, and the impact of US tariff policies on India's export-import dynamics; connects to GS Paper 2 (IR) and GS Paper 3 (Economy/Trade).
key_facts: India reportedly paused US trade deal talks over tariff disagreements
Trump administration's tariff regime cited as the key sticking point
Trump praised PM Modi's long tenure as beneficial for US-India trade
Bilateral trade deal between India and US has been under negotiation for years

title: US–Iran Nuclear Talks to Continue in Switzerland
summary: Following preliminary progress, the US Special Envoy and Iran's Foreign Minister are set to meet in Switzerland for the next round of nuclear negotiations. The talks are aimed at reaching a new framework to limit Iran's nuclear programme in exchange for sanctions relief, reviving the spirit of the 2015 JCPOA (Joint Comprehensive Plan of Action). The venue — Switzerland — is traditionally used for sensitive diplomatic back-channel negotiations given its neutral status. The outcome of these talks carries implications for global energy markets and West Asian geopolitics.
exam_angle: Relevant to West Asia geopolitics, nuclear non-proliferation (NPT framework), and India's energy security given its dependence on West Asian oil; also connects to the JCPOA and multilateral diplomacy.
key_facts: US and Iran to hold next round of nuclear talks in Switzerland
Talks aimed at reviving a framework similar to the 2015 JCPOA
Switzerland chosen for its traditionally neutral diplomatic status
Implications for global oil prices and India's energy security

title: Nobel Laureate John Jumper Leaves Google DeepMind for Anthropic
summary: John Jumper, the 2024 Nobel Prize winner in Chemistry for his work on AlphaFold (AI-based protein structure prediction), has resigned from Google DeepMind and joined AI safety company Anthropic. Jumper's move is significant as AlphaFold revolutionised structural biology by accurately predicting 3D protein structures, an achievement considered a breakthrough for drug discovery and life sciences. Anthropic, co-founded by former OpenAI executives, is known for developing the Claude series of AI models and focuses on AI safety research. The shift highlights talent movement between leading AI research organisations globally.
exam_angle: Connects to Nobel Prize 2024 (Chemistry), AI applications in science (AlphaFold/protein folding), and the global AI talent landscape — relevant for Sci-Tech current affairs and 'Persons in News'.
key_facts: John Jumper: 2024 Nobel Prize in Chemistry (AlphaFold – protein structure prediction)
Previously at Google DeepMind; now joined Anthropic
AlphaFold: AI tool that predicts 3D protein structures — major breakthrough in biology
Anthropic: AI safety company, developer of Claude AI models

title: Chinese AI Models Dominate Global Top 10 by Token Usage
summary: A new analysis reveals that Chinese artificial intelligence models account for the majority share among the global top 10 AI models ranked by token usage — a key metric of real-world AI deployment and adoption. This signals a major shift in the global AI landscape, with Chinese models increasingly competing with US counterparts such as OpenAI's GPT series and Anthropic's Claude. The development has geopolitical implications, particularly in the context of US export controls on advanced semiconductors to China. It also raises questions about AI governance, data sovereignty, and digital standards.
exam_angle: Relevant to global AI governance, technology competition between the US and China, and India's AI policy ambitions under the National AI Mission; connects to GS Paper 3 (Sci-Tech) and GS Paper 2 (IR — technology geopolitics).
key_facts: Chinese AI models hold majority share in global top 10 by token usage
Token usage is a key metric for real-world AI model deployment
Challenges US dominance in global AI (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google)
Context: US semiconductor export controls to China still in effect

title: India's Economy Shows Resilience to West Asia Geopolitical Shock
summary: An analysis in The Indian Express highlights how India's economy has demonstrated resilience to the economic shock arising from the West Asia conflict, including disruptions to Red Sea shipping routes and elevated oil prices. Key factors cited include India's diversified energy sourcing, robust domestic demand, and prudent fiscal management by the government and RBI. Despite a temporary spike in crude oil import costs, India's current account deficit and foreign exchange reserves remained manageable. The analysis underscores structural strengths that have buffered the Indian economy from external shocks.
exam_angle: Relevant to India's macroeconomic resilience, current account deficit management, and India's vulnerability to oil price shocks — important for GS Paper 3 (Economy) and Essay paper on Indian economic strengths.
key_facts: West Asia conflict disrupted Red Sea shipping, raising India's oil import costs
India's foreign exchange reserves and CAD remained largely stable
Diversified energy sourcing cited as a key buffer
RBI's monetary management and domestic demand supported resilience
